ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a tiramisu entry (FOODON_03315091) used as the closest anchor for the cake component; the full set (cake + tall drink) has no exact FoodOn composite entry.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers tiramisu as a dessert category; the bundled drink component is not separately coded here. Composite set meal has no exact FoodEx2 entry.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a commercial tiramisu cake and coffee drink set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Starbucks Singapore set comprising one tiramisu cake slice (approximately 130 g) and one tall (12 fl oz / 355 ml) coffee beverage, totalling approximately 380 g combined weight.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake + Tall Drink Set — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this Starbucks set (cr_id G00010280). All macros, micronutrients, and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated at 12.5 g/100g based on the known composition of mascarpone, cream, and butter in tiramisu as documented in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for tiramisu (NDB 19921) and mascarpone (NDB 01019), cross-referenced against the HPB total fat of 23.86 g/100g.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Starbucks set. GI of 62 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for tiramisu and coffee-based dessert beverages. Tiramisu GI is reported at approximately 60–65 in the Sydney GI Database (analogous dessert category, glucose reference = 100). A midpoint of 62 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (62) × carbohydrate per serving for the cake slice only (approximately 130 g, yielding 38.3 g carbohydrate) plus the drink component (approximately 250 ml, yielding ~32.5 g carbohydrate from a standard sweetened tall latte), giving a combined carbohydrate of approximately 70.6 g per set. GL = 62 × 70.6 / 100 = 43.8. This is a high glycemic load per set serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (18.7 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (0.6 g/100g) and moderate sodium (173 mg/100g); sugar-sweetened beverage component independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this set due to its high sugar content (18.7 g/100g) and estimated saturated fat (~12.5 g/100g), both of which contribute to arterial stiffness and elevated cardiovascular risk independent of sodium; if consumed, request an unsweetened beverage substitution and limit the cake portion to a small taste.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 18.7 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g diabetes threshold; dietary fibre only 0.6 g/100g provides negligible glucose-buffering; GI estimated at 62 with a high glycemic load of ~43.8 per set serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: This set is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes; the high sugar content (18.7 g/100g), minimal fibre (0.6 g/100g), and high glycemic load (~43.8 per set) will cause a rapid and sustained postprandial glucose spike, and the set should be avoided or replaced with a sugar-free beverage and a lower-carbohydrate snack.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g from mascarpone, cream, and butter; cholesterol 121 mg/100g; high sugar (18.7 g/100g) promotes dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this set; the estimated saturated fat of ~12.5 g/100g substantially exceeds the AHA daily limit when consumed as a full set serving, and the combination of high saturated fat, dietary cholesterol (121 mg/100g), and high sugar promotes LDL elevation, triglyceride accumulation, and endothelial dysfunction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g from cream and mascarpone exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; high sugar (18.7 g/100g) further promotes inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this set; the high saturated fat from mascarpone and cream (~12.5 g/100g) promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, and the high sugar content (18.7 g/100g) further amplifies systemic inflammation, worsening joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake and Tall Drink Set is a commercial food-and-beverage bundle sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets, typically available from 2 PM daily at a promotional price of SGD 14.90. The set comprises one slice of tiramisu cake and one tall (12 fl oz) coffee beverage. The tiramisu cake is an Italian-origin no-bake dessert made from layers of espresso-soaked ladyfinger biscuits (savoiardi) and a rich mascarpone cream filling, dusted with cocoa powder. The texture is soft, creamy, and moist, with a pronounced coffee bitterness balanced by the sweetness of the mascarpone layer and sugar. The tall drink component is typically an espresso-based coffee beverage such as a latte or cappuccino. Nutritionally, this set is energy-dense at 353 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 23.86 g/100g (estimated saturated fat ~12.5 g/100g from mascarpone and cream), high sugar at 18.7 g/100g, and moderate carbohydrates at 29.44 g/100g. Dietary fibre is very low at 0.6 g/100g. Sodium is 173 mg/100g. Cholesterol is elevated at 121 mg/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium) with a high glycemic load of approximately 43.8 per set serving, making it clinically unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The set is not halal-certified at all Starbucks Singapore outlets and contains allergens including milk, eggs, and gluten. It is best consumed as an occasional treat and is not recommended as a regular dietary component for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: Is the Starbucks tiramisu cake set healthy?
How many calories in Starbucks tiramisu cake set Singapore?
Can diabetics eat Starbucks tiramisu cake?
Is Starbucks tiramisu cake halal in Singapore?
What is the sugar content of Starbucks tiramisu cake set?
Is Starbucks tiramisu cake suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010280, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy tiramisu and mascarpone entries as the HPB record does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for tiramisu-class desserts. Overall confidence is high for macros and secondary nutrients; medium for saturated fat disaggregation and GI.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (12.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for tiramisu (NDB 19921) and mascarpone (NDB 01019) as the HPB source does not report fatty acid fractions separately. This estimate is consistent with the known composition of mascarpone-cream-based tiramisu. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific Starbucks set are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for tiramisu-class desserts; no direct GI measurement for this specific Starbucks product is available in any approved source. 4. Halal status: Starbucks Singapore is not universally halal-certified; is_halal is set to false as a conservative default. 5. Serving size of 380 g is an estimate based on a standard Starbucks tall drink (approximately 250 ml / ~250 g) plus a typical tiramisu cake slice (approximately 130 g); no official combined set weight is published by Starbucks Singapore or HPB. 6. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Beverages / Coffee' (input) to 'Desserts / Cake and beverage set meal' to better reflect the composite nature of this set, which is primarily a dessert-and-drink bundle rather than a standalone beverage. 7. Q8 self-check performed (iteration 3): all string values in aliases_by_language have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. English aliases: 'Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake + Tall Drink Set', 'Starbucks Coffee Tiramisu Cake and Tall Drink Set', 'Starbucks Tiramisu Cake Set', 'Starbucks Tiramisu Cake + Drink Set', 'Starbucks 2PM Set', 'Starbucks afternoon set tiramisu' — all present in flat aliases. Chinese aliases: '星巴克咖啡提拉米苏蛋糕+大杯饮料套餐', '星巴克咖啡提拉米蘇蛋糕+大杯飲料套餐' — both present. Malay: 'Set Kek Tiramisu Kopi Starbucks + Minuman Tall' — present. Tamil: 'ஸ்டார்பக்ஸ் காபி திரமிசு கேக் + டால் பானம் செட்' — present. Hindi: 'स्टारबक्स कॉफी तिरामिसु केक + टॉल ड्रिंक सेट' — present. Romanized: 'Xīng bā kè kā fēi tí lā mǐ sū dàn gāo + dà bēi yǐn liào tào cān' — present. All 12 entries confirmed consistent. Note: the previous iteration draft contained a Unicode inconsistency in the Hindi aliases_by_language entry ('तिरामिसu' mixing Latin 'u' with Devanagari); this has been corrected to fully Devanagari 'तिरामिसु' in both aliases_by_language and the flat aliases array in this iteration.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built from HPB input data; multilingual names added for Singaporean cuisine requirements.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'; added romanized Chinese alias to flat aliases array to resolve q8 failure.

Iteration 3: Corrected Unicode inconsistency in Hindi aliases_by_language entry ('तिरामिसu' mixed script corrected to fully Devanagari 'तिरामिसु'); performed exhaustive verbatim self-check of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases array; confirmed all 12 entries are present verbatim in flat aliases; updated notes with detailed q8 self-check audit trail.
